New York’s biggest developments center on housing, labor and governance: major affordable-housing and infrastructure plans face resistance or new funding pushes, while labor tensions span possible doormen and transit disputes even as NYU faculty settled a strike. City Hall is also under pressure over policing, immigration/ICE cooperation and budget stewardship.

### Risks & Alerts

- Fire is blocking a Lincoln Tunnel lane from New York City to New Jersey, causing likely traffic delays. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- A possible strike by 34,000 doormen and building workers could disrupt services in many NYC residential buildings. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMipAFBVV95cUxNVEc2dVVCLXU4U2FfYWc5cjhJYjJQbmZBdmNKU281RXVrNWpOdjJCY3doR2NGRm0yWnRzV0hXbzhqbzdoMVUtb3FmTU1UTXJHdnNZRjN6dXBXQndMWm5IMDhMak1PbzJtZ2pqMWRjYkFsX2V3TmwwM3VvamJETndiYVhJSnRDdlZEU0lESmh4eG1GRmk4N3hYTE90akdWOUdMLWtidg?oc=5)
- NYCHA’s $1.2 billion Chelsea redevelopment is being delayed by 24 seniors resisting demolition and relocation plans. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- State officials are considering $350 million to restart the stalled Atlantic Yards platform project in Brooklyn. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisgFBVV95cUxNOEpybTdtRFMxTXdRN0REREh4WXp0UjJ5WjJZM1Q4MkJzOFZqWkx0VXBHMnRmdUZYaFpnR1BDbHk3S04tUmM5NlNVdEJFeXpsQUhRSldPb2wweERvbXZ3V3Fiei0zM0xBWEh3MGdlVkR1ZERPTnJpM2RGSy1NNUZBUUZpVjMySjEtUlN2S1lwOUV5ZWZiRmdPck10SGNoQXpjbXBIVUh4eEM2cmdwSVRhRlFR?oc=5)
- Mayor Mamdani announced a new affordable housing initiative in Brooklyn after backing away from a campaign promise. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Albany immigration legislation is stalled over whether local agencies can cooperate with ICE. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMipAFBVV95cUxQUjJwU1lHYUxHaE1KWTEzalRuaGtDcTVDWHlYdHB1bnQya1RJTFVTd1JqemVUa2hkUm9oLW9FUFF2NjNCNFMxM0RoTVl1Zlhsbk1GSnlHUmxQM19DMGQzclpOcExxbFRwY21udWJranVzamhmWnBkUFhXTTEtRW95dHlnNFVaVm5rLWFyR2lScFA0Nk4zc1lXVTNQS0ZzNkdRU0JpTA?oc=5)
- Some NYC officials want to expand the five-person immigration office amid a broader ICE crackdown. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Mayor Mamdani is reportedly highly unlikely to pursue a property tax hike. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMizwFBVV95cUxPVnFaem9KNkJETGVJc2NQWTRLOHU0LW9vMG1xMlMyZlB5QThlcGZMZGRNSDh2bUEzcWtLRTQ0OGxrcnpVU0NMenduZHBQODkzcDdUSENOZEJYQmlzaENsOFdKV0hxd0FLczBDOGNjb3lfbDFGQ3B6VG4wVUVOemZsbXJHTm9mYXpSbXZFdWs5eC13WkVJRkV3N2J5d2xJd3NzR01EMGpndHdkLUU2R2V3WHRaVk5GUjVmR0laMkpIS1J6YTZhNHdkYjgtVmVaOW_?oc=5)
- Work on a controversial Staten Island street-widening project is set to begin in coming weeks. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- NYU faculty reached an agreement ending their strike, restoring normal university operations. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijgFBVV95cUxQSm1zN0lFLUxpMU04Qm9MenBtVW0wVnRlY3N5UXdZS1oyYlJETzFDM2JfeEZDVlNSU2NsZXhMUVR5SUhjWGx3V1hEZFBRV2hmMzYzcWhvRkFrbVI3RklLbWpDLWJPTGNaeExvQkhuZmxmVk5NT0o3WFpuUDR6YVJMcmhyMkp0aFhESjM4alZn?oc=5)

### Opportunities & Welfare

- Thousands of New Yorkers risk losing rent assistance funds, with no city or state fix yet announced. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiiwFBVV95cUxQWXp1bW0xaTZueU9FU3lMWFdJd0c0Wk51d3JZMzF5bTJxQnNWVTNPc29WQzI4V2pxdkllTnRaT05ka2tqYzh2RWlGbk82bFFCbkctWlFHSXg5bklsR09vNlN2dGdwU1M5X25OaWN0TjRkanFPUjZpZWRSUGxnY01EVDlDakM3RnM2Vi1v?oc=5)
- NYC opened a new anti-trauma intake center for homeless women seeking shelter services. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMinwFBVV95cUxQX3psQlk2clloVWxUakxGemdZSjRFZUxLY3V3UXJ0NUJrWl91ZWFuREljVTZmRVA1d0J0QUJwS2trTU4zbkt2bXpuNkJhTlBWYjQtV0Q1VXRTTHQ1bmVYeHpqUGN2dGp1N0VxWDJPMkpNNkswWktJQ0gtYWRlZ1lid1Y2QzdZN2d6ZENIYnVXT3dUeFlqd0NVaFBjbTFudHc?oc=5)
- Apply soon for Bronx and Brooklyn housing lotteries offering apartments starting at $940 monthly. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMinAFBVV95cUxPbXJLRW1FUnlNd0E2X1d5TE5EMTh0OG1zMFllVTQ2ckMtZ0xUWlJvcEJ6XzFCYnhyN0VhcmVtMTFHLUdHRVR0bE91VDh2Ml9HR0dTOWlkdUhleEhScjNWel9FZ3pkdVlRb0hKYXNPQ3ZselI3WlhZcHFidVlJR3haTEVZZENJNHg4YncyMGJlRTVvY2dIT3UyNldiVDk?oc=5)
- NYC schools received new AI-use guidance that could affect classroom assignments and student technology access. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMikgFBVV95cUxPcVVSUXAycUhySTQydV9vUEp3OUV0SExlZ3owQTY4Q2lzOXZ0NnJvbkR2V1lSMHFUanYxYllGVlA0MHFoNkU4ek5NOHZIVXZMTHVHMXdnZzhSWDFZU0FCdzMxRmY5X2dPNXdhR0I3LVFkdEVsY0dCMnAtY2Y2WGZHdlc0eC16MHpLeUFVRGJoSl9oQQ?oc=5)

### Community

- Ninth Avenue will get a “super sidewalk” and major streetscape upgrades affecting Hell’s Kitchen pedestrians and traffic. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisAFBVV95cUxNbGxRYjZCdVE0UW8xZW5pT0ltZ1E1djdobDlseDhJT3huQjR6eG1FbzZGNno2N0cyM1BfellRem1uczdMSkpHSUtDRjh2cF9yaE9BOXFQNUplRnRqMEhTVjBXOTI4RW9pY3d0M2ZiemxodmJyVy1pemRfdENvUjhxZmpQVHlPenh2RkNTZ2FjY2FwVEt0UHltYy0yaDN1V3hscVhITU1zd29SUkwwYXJCQQ?oc=5)
- Luna Park opens for the 2026 season with Coney Island opening weekend events for families and visitors. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMieEFVX3lxTFBaYm5hcFQyZEJwbXlmWW5sOERzTTY3all0d1pKQ1JoNmlncVRDdUhudEt6T0k3X0Z0ekFKa1ZOaHhQWksxY3JWR1hjWEJqT2pEVWtXdmdOdlpjaTdsS1FkVkpQckJzekNCYlkyRVRMSkl5bUhCMU1HaQ?oc=5)
- A new waterfront public space has opened in NYC, creating another outdoor destination for residents. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Union Square is getting its only rooftop bar above the W, adding a new nightlife option nearby. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMizgFBVV95cUxOdVdlTmxOM2ZaZlotNEQyenYzeFd2MXlDeW5FUUV6dFEyd2V1TE16Sk1sYXFIOGQyTnVsRURLOUFUVFc1MGZ5ZTREenVjaU5IVFZLVV9YUWZWQXFhbmdJWW4yTFpvMzdiRW1BNEhSSERXcld0VGM0UHhsOUxFZHEwakE3SDQyTkhzbFlmY3RvWXAxcUlaMVB3U19PZkdHUEpDZnJEN01SREJEaDc5V3kxajhPZlR0QWRZQkZSNkR5WFpXMUhmS29fbkkwNjNfUQ?oc=5)
- Corima is opening a more casual Mexican bar on the Lower East Side, expanding neighborhood dining options. - A new all-female kaiseki counter is drawing attention as a notable addition to New York’s dining scene.
